Don't be Ungrateful person！！！！

Don’t be someone who takes kindness for granted.
When someone treats you well, it’s not something you’re entitled to.
Ask yourself honestly can you give the same in return?

If you can’t, then at least appreciate it.
Because people who give their heart… can also feel deeply hurt and disappointed.

Some people wait for the rain to stop.
Others learn how to walk in the rain.

I used to live rushing through everything tired, pulled between responsibilities.
The things I wanted to do? I’d wait until I had “time.”
The people I wanted to see? I’d wait until I wasn’t “so tired.”

Then I realized
life doesn’t wait for you to be ready.

What you think is “waiting for the right moment”
is often just slowly missing what actually matters.

Now, I don’t wait for perfection, the right mood, or everything to be in place.
I just go. I do. I show up.

Even if it’s not perfect, it’s still more real than standing still.

Because real freedom isn’t when life gets easier
it’s when you finally have the courage to live it your way.

（Sometimes walking into the rain
is not a choice,
but a quiet form of protection
a place where tears can finally hide.）

#LifeAwakening #StopWaiting #TakeAction #JustDoIt #LiveInTheMoment #ImperfectIsOkay #GrowthJourney #RealLife #SelfBreakthrough #LifeChoices #BeBrave #NoMoreProcrastination #youcandobetter

The Nike Air More Uptempo turns 30 this year! This classic designed by the icon Wilson Smith is one all time favorites. Back in 1996, I was entering 8th grade and my mom was on a strict $100 or less budget for new school shoes. I obviously wasn’t going to be getting More, but thanks to Nike and their takedowns I was more than happy having Much Uptempos! After Scottie debuted More in the ‘96 playoffs, we saw both pairs in the Olympics with Charles Barkley and Gary Payton wearing them as well. Do you have a pair of More Uptempo’s in your collection currently?

caption:
This little punk tried to scam me yesterday. He listed these Nike x Stussy sweat pants as authentic. On closer inspection they were clearly fake:

❌ The typography here was off — the “STUSSY” font is incorrect and too generic.
❌ The zipper moulding looked cheap finish was overly shiny and the shape didn’t match standard Nike Tech or Sportswear zip pulls.
❌ The taper and leg cut were inconsistent with authentic Nike joggers.
❌ The fleece texture was lighter and lower density than typical Nike Sportswear fleece.
❌ Stitch lines and panel placement were slightly off.

Just goes to show that not everything is as it seems and you need to be on your toes checking everything your source.

#scammer #fakenike #trustnoone

POV: You're at a flea market and spot a Cartier Love bracelet for $200.

Here's how to know in 60 seconds if it's real or a $30 fake from Shenzhen:

0–15 sec → Pick it up. Real 18K gold feels heavy. If it feels light or plasticky? Walk away.

15–30 sec → Find the hallmark. Look for "750" (18K gold) stamped crisply on the metal. Blurry, shaky, or missing? Fake.

30–45 sec → Test the clasp. Luxury clasps snap shut with authority. A fake one fumbles.

45–60 sec → Check the box. Cartier red = embossed logo, not printed. Tiffany blue = one specific Pantone. Wrong shade? Red flag. 🚩

And if you're spending $500+? Always get it authenticated first. Always.

Fake luxury jewellery seizures in Canada are up 40%+ since 2020. The fakes are getting good. Don't get burned.

Save this before your next secondhand find.

caption:
New details regarding #Nike’s ongoing lawsuit against #StockX were just uncovered this week, accusing the resale platform of knowingly selling #counterfeit sneakers and the inability to determine if products coming into its facilities are authentic. 

It’s worth noting that the documents were filed back in August but were unsealed this week. In the suit, Nike attacks StockX’s #authenticity claims, saying that the #resale giant admitted that its authenticators could not determine if a product is authentic, passed counterfeit footwear as authentic, and it had no idea of the percentage of #fakesneakers that made it through authentication and into consumers’s hands.

@newbalancelifestyle U992MM

GR ‘Mushroom’ 🤎🌖🪨🍄‍🟫🩶
MOONROCK / MUSHROOM

This ‘Mushroom’ 992 was released in March 2025 for @newbalance’s #MADEinUSA SS ‘25 collection and marked the 992’s return from a 3-year hiatus. The 992 was designed by Jonathan Bacon and originally debuted in 2006 for the brand’s centenary, but it was shelved for 10+ years from 2011-2020 and from 2022-2025.

Five (5) core 992 cws were available in early March 2025, but this was the first 992 GR in Season 7 of @teddysantis as Creative Director for NB’s MiUSA division. I’ve posted these sneakers before, but I wanted to post shots from better angles and some standalone photos of them. I’ve copied some of my caption in my old post above / below.

This pair sports a light shade of grey with subtle tan undertones named ‘Moonrock’, as well as earthy lighter / darker ‘Mushroom’ accents. It features a variety of materials from shaggy / smooth suede to leather (or synthetic material), mesh, and 3M reflective material.

‘Moonrock’ overlays composed of shaggy suede cover the mudguard, trim (under the quarter panels), and heel counter. Smooth suede is utilized for two shades of ‘Mushroom’ accents; the tongue / keeper loop and heel tab feature a lighter shade ‘Mushroom,’ while darker ‘Mushroom’ accents are on the vamp and around / below the collar on the medial / lateral sides.

The quarter panels and eyestays are composed of ‘Moonrock’ leather (or synthetic material?). 3M reflective material is visible on the medial / lateral quarter panels in the top layer of the silver ‘N’ logos, which are embroidered over white ‘N’-shaped leather (or synthetic material?) adhered to the quarter panels to create white outlines for the ‘N’ logos.

The light grey / brown tones throughout the upper are complemented by lighter grey / beige mesh underlays on the tongue and vamp, as well as around the quarters and collar on the medial / lateral sides. More silver reflective 3M hits appear beneath the throat (between the eyestays) and below the medial / lateral sides of the collar near the heel with ‘992’ branding on the lateral side.

I’ve run out of room as usual, so the rest of the caption is continued in comments 👇.
